\"path\": \"lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js\", \"symbol\": \"makeSafe\"}\n  ],\n  \"text\": \"Node.js core implements comprehensive prototype pollution defense mechanisms across its codebase. The primary defense strategy is located in lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js, which exports a makeSafe() function (lines 392-402) that creates frozen, null-prototype safe versions of built-in objects and their iterators.\\n\\nKey Defense Patterns:\\n\\n1. Object.create(null) Pattern: Used throughout core modules to create objects without prototype chains, preventing __proto__ poisoning. Examples include:\\n   - lib/v8.js: detailLevelDict and vmConstants with __proto__: null\\n   - lib/_http_agent.js: options objects with __proto__: null (lines 96, 305, 365)\\n   - lib/internal/timers.js: timerListMap and knownTimersById with __proto__: null\\n   - lib/internal/fs/utils.js: lazyDateFields with nested __proto__: null patterns\\n   - lib/internal/quic/quic.js: block objects with __proto__: null\\n\\n2. Safe Built-in Classes: The primordials system exports Safe* versions:\\n   - SafeMap: Used in fs.js, inspector.js, internal/url.js, and 70+ other files\\n   - SafeSet: Used in fs.js, repl.js, internal/url.js, child_process.js, and 25+ other files\\n   - SafeWeakMap: Used in domain.js, internal/util.js, internal/event_target.js, and 15+ other files\\n   - SafeWeakRef and SafeFinalizationRegistry: Used for cleanup operations\\n   - SafePromise variants: SafePromiseAll, SafePromiseRace, SafePromisePrototypeFinally\\n\\n3. hasOwnProperty Defense: The codebase defensively uses ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty checks:\\n   - Imported as primordial in buffer.js, cluster.js, and many core modules\\n   - Prevents reliance on potentially-polluted prototype chains\\n\\n4. ESLint Enforcement: lib/eslint.config_partial.mjs enforces the 'node-core/avoid-prototype-pollution' rule, with specific disable directives in:\\n   - lib/internal/net.js (lines 40, 51) - acceptable perf regression trade-offs\\n   - lib/internal/main/print_help.js (line 36) - uses SafeMap with array initialization\\n   - lib/internal/webstreams/readablestream.js (lines 499, 511, 515) - returns object literals\\n\\n5. Core Module Usage: All major core modules that handle external data defensively use primordials:\\n   - HTTP modules: lib/_http_agent.js, lib/_http_outgoing.js, lib/internal/http2/core.js, lib/internal/http2/util.js\\n   - Crypto: lib/internal/crypto/x509.js, lib/internal/crypto/ec.js, lib/internal/crypto/keys.js\\n   - URL parsing: lib/internal/url.js with SafeMap and SafeSet\\n   - WebIDL: lib/internal/webidl.js with SafeSet for enum validation\\n   - Worker/Async: lib/internal/worker.js, lib/internal/async_context_frame.js\\n   - File system: lib/fs.js, lib/internal/fs/glob.js, lib/internal/fs/utils.js, lib/internal/fs/rimraf.js\\n\\nThe defense-in-depth approach ensures that even if user input manipulates __proto__, constructor, or other properties, the core runtime remains secure through the use of frozen prototypes and safe built-in alternatives that have been isolated from global prototype mutations.\"\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":12,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3272,"cache_read_input_tokens":64926,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":3272},"output_tokens":6,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"68536a3d-b195-45c5-828b-a41dea75759d","uuid":"ab1c3db8-a3b2-4efd-811e-e48e68f3bd96"}

\"path\": \"lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js\", \"symbol\": \"makeSafe\"}\n  ],\n  \"text\": \"Node.js core implements comprehensive prototype pollution defense mechanisms across its codebase. The primary defense strategy is located in lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js, which exports a makeSafe() function (lines 392-402) that creates frozen, null-prototype safe versions of built-in objects and their iterators.\\n\\nKey Defense Patterns:\\n\\n1. Object.create(null) Pattern: Used throughout core modules to create objects without prototype chains, preventing __proto__ poisoning. Examples include:\\n   - lib/v8.js: detailLevelDict and vmConstants with __proto__: null\\n   - lib/_http_agent.js: options objects with __proto__: null (lines 96, 305, 365)\\n   - lib/internal/timers.js: timerListMap and knownTimersById with __proto__: null\\n   - lib/internal/fs/utils.js: lazyDateFields with nested __proto__: null patterns\\n   - lib/internal/quic/quic.js: block objects with __proto__: null\\n\\n2. Safe Built-in Classes: The primordials system exports Safe* versions:\\n   - SafeMap: Used in fs.js, inspector.js, internal/url.js, and 70+ other files\\n   - SafeSet: Used in fs.js, repl.js, internal/url.js, child_process.js, and 25+ other files\\n   - SafeWeakMap: Used in domain.js, internal/util.js, internal/event_target.js, and 15+ other files\\n   - SafeWeakRef and SafeFinalizationRegistry: Used for cleanup operations\\n   - SafePromise variants: SafePromiseAll, SafePromiseRace, SafePromisePrototypeFinally\\n\\n3. hasOwnProperty Defense: The codebase defensively uses ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty checks:\\n   - Imported as primordial in buffer.js, cluster.js, and many core modules\\n   - Prevents reliance on potentially-polluted prototype chains\\n\\n4. ESLint Enforcement: lib/eslint.config_partial.mjs enforces the 'node-core/avoid-prototype-pollution' rule, with specific disable directives in:\\n   - lib/internal/net.js (lines 40, 51) - acceptable perf regression trade-offs\\n   - lib/internal/main/print_help.js (line 36) - uses SafeMap with array initialization\\n   - lib/internal/webstreams/readablestream.js (lines 499, 511, 515) - returns object literals\\n\\n5. Core Module Usage: All major core modules that handle external data defensively use primordials:\\n   - HTTP modules: lib/_http_agent.js, lib/_http_outgoing.js, lib/internal/http2/core.js, lib/internal/http2/util.js\\n   - Crypto: lib/internal/crypto/x509.js, lib/internal/crypto/ec.js, lib/internal/crypto/keys.js\\n   - URL parsing: lib/internal/url.js with SafeMap and SafeSet\\n   - WebIDL: lib/internal/webidl.js with SafeSet for enum validation\\n   - Worker/Async: lib/internal/worker.js, lib/internal/async_context_frame.js\\n   - File system: lib/fs.js, lib/internal/fs/glob.js, lib/internal/fs/utils.js, lib/internal/fs/rimraf.js\\n\\nThe defense-in-depth approach ensures that even if user input manipulates __proto__, constructor, or other properties, the core runtime remains secure through the use of frozen prototypes and safe built-in alternatives that have been isolated from global prototype mutations.\"\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

Creating prototype-less objects in ~30 files\n   - Used in HTTP, crypto, file system, URL handling modules\n   - Examples: `__proto__: null` literals in lib/v8.js, lib/_http_agent.js, lib/internal/timers.js\n\n2. **Safe Built-in Classes** -
